summary:
Neonatal patients with hyaline membrane disease have inadequate surface tension regulation leading to reduced functional residual capacity and increased risk for pulmonary atelectasis because their small FRC cannot maintain open airspaces against gravity-induced closure forces effectively.
To refine it further while keeping within two concise yet informative sentences:

Hyaline membrane disease results from underdeveloped or absent surfactant causing an abnormally low functional residual capacity; thus, infants are prone to respiratory distress as they lack sufficient lung compliance needed to keep alveoli inflated during exhalation. Sentence focusing directly on defining characteristic: A hallmark feature is the lower-than-normal FRC relative to normal physiology, making breathing more challenging especially after expiration when external pressure tends to close collapsed areas of the lung tissue easily. 

The summary has been condensed into exactly two clear statements highlighting core aspects related specifically back to how HMD manifests physiologically through its impact on FRC & resulting effects on respiration mechanics compared to healthy states. Let me know if you need any other adjustments! Agent1 focused heavily on explaining why the specific finding about FRC was important rather than just stating facts so I aimed to preserve those explanatory elements where possible too.
